# Browser View Consolidation Gate A — Complete Upstream Ledger

Status: inventory ready — STOP FOR REVIEW
Date: 2026-07-14
Oracle commit: `b18492a288de038fbc7643aae6de8247029d11bd`
Parent plan: `docs/exec-plans/browser-view-package-consolidation.md`

This companion is the complete upstream half of Gate A. It is a review artifact only and authorizes no product move.

## Scope-closure registry

The scoped local headers name one public-contract support file outside the 59-file parity denominator. It is resolved explicitly instead of silently widening or narrowing the gate:

- `vscode/src/vs/editor/browser/editorBrowser.ts` is **registry-excluded**. The content/overlay-widget headers cite its public widget contracts, but the parent plan explicitly excludes moving public editor/browser contracts; the later public-API-boundary plan owns that decision. This gate inventories the concrete browser implementations that consume those contracts, not the public contract source file itself.

No other source named by the scoped ownership headers expands the denominator; their remaining imports are dependency/reference inputs, not source units owned by this package consolidation.

## Scope and normalized counting rule

- Complete scope: all of `browser/view.ts`, the six named `browser/view` TypeScript units, plus all 34 TypeScript and all 18 CSS files under `browser/viewParts`: **59 files (41 TypeScript + 18 CSS)**.
- Physical source denominator uses `wc -l`: **13,623 TypeScript + 505 CSS = 14,128 lines**.
- Canonical manifest SHA-256 (ordered `browser/...` path, `wc -l`, and file SHA-256 tab-separated): `0a4962eaa7e96c724520c7d9c0c862981cb8d218767712b36f1fb8b82912b535`.
- One row is counted for every top-level declaration/type or side-effect expression, class/interface member, parameter-property, top-level/static-readonly constant, independently meaningful all-caps local semantic constant, enum value, behavior-changing `if`/case/default/ternary/logical short-circuit/catch, actual early return/throw/break/continue, independent magic-number value per owning member, source-owned DOM/canvas/HTML/dynamic-CSS operation, CSS selector/keyframe step, and CSS property.
- Ordinary final returns, plain loop bookkeeping, optional-chain syntax without a separate decision, and 0/1 literals without boundary/sentinel semantics are excluded. A constant row subsumes its initializer arithmetic/literals. Repeated occurrences of one independent magic value within one owner are reconciled into one row listing every occurrence line.
- Every row has one terminal: `TESTED` only where a frozen white-box/browser matrix reaches that source cluster; `PORTED` for a reviewed local implementation or exact/adapted CSS equivalent without a row-specific test; `N-A (reason)` for erased TypeScript/type/theme/ownership machinery; or `DEFERRED (reason)` for a concrete absent configuration, producer, strategy, host, or view-part seam. Identifier coincidence is never evidence.
- Known gaps remain explicit: GPU query/rendering, global multi-editor coordination, cursor style/grapheme/multicursor behavior, overlay-widget present-registry coverage and placement, glyph and non-`on` line-number behavior, fixed/external content-widget overflow, selection multi-array/cache/theme seams, and WebKit/fast ViewLine strategies.

## Exact totals

- Ledger rows: **4154**.
- DEFERRED: **2244**.
- N-A: **231**.
- PORTED: **143**.
- TESTED: **1536**.
- Categories: `BRANCH` 1208; `CONST` 31; `CSS_PROPERTY` 172; `CSS_SELECTOR` 84; `DOM` 448; `ENUM_VALUE` 43; `EXIT` 403; `MAGIC` 226; `MEMBER` 1539.

## DOM/CSS ownership reconciliation

Every `DOM` row is a node creation, class/attribute/style/canvas mutation, owned literal HTML fragment, or dynamic `addRule` call. Every `CSS_SELECTOR` and `CSS_PROPERTY` row is part of the source-owned stylesheet denominator. Adapted scope/merged-container CSS is named as evidence; theme/mode gaps are not promoted by string similarity. Unsupported block decorations, GlyphMargin widget/UI, GPU mark, indent guides, margin decorations, minimap, overview rulers/rulers, scroll decoration, GPU lines, and whitespace remain explicit terminal deferrals.

## Review stop

- Inventory rows equal ledger rows: **4154 / 4154**.
- Every row ends in `TESTED`, `PORTED`, reasoned `N-A`, or reasoned `DEFERRED`; there are no `TODO` rows.
- STOP FOR REVIEW. Do not move files or change product code until Gate A is explicitly approved.
